title: [10] Add a Prometheus /metrics endpoint to the dashboard for external scraping
why_we_need_this: The dashboard exposes its runtime signals (CPU, memory, per-peer WireGuard traffic, uptime, peer online/offline, last-handshake, alert state) only through its HTML UI and JSON `/api/*` endpoints, which are shaped for the dashboard's own rendering — not for time-series scraping. There is no way to pull these into an external monitoring stack. Disk usage in particular is computed internally for the high-disk alert but is not queryable anywhere (this was the original motivation: disk visibility gap). Separately, the root `README.md` already advertises "A Prometheus `/metrics` endpoint is exposed for external scraping" — but no such endpoint exists yet, so the docs currently overstate the product. Adding a standard Prometheus `/metrics` endpoint lets operators scrape the VPN host into Prometheus/Grafana over the tunnel, closes the disk-visibility gap, and makes the README truthful.
acceptance_criteria:
- Expose a new `GET /metrics` endpoint on the dashboard's HTTP server returning the Prometheus text exposition format via `github.com/prometheus/client_golang` (`promhttp.Handler()`), with the library pinned to an EXACT version in `dashboard/go.mod` (repo convention: no `~>`/ranges).
- `/metrics` is ADDITIVE and separate: do NOT change any existing `/api/*` JSON response shape, and do NOT add these metrics to the MCP server / MCP tool surface. `/metrics` is the only new surface introduced.
- Export, at minimum, the signals the dashboard already collects, as properly-named Prometheus metric families (use a `wireguard_*` prefix, `_bytes`/`_seconds` unit suffixes, and per-peer `name`/`pubkey` labels where relevant): CPU load, memory usage, system uptime, per-peer RX/TX bytes, per-peer last-handshake age, total peer count and online-peer count, and current alert state.
- Include disk usage as a metric (e.g. `wireguard_disk_used_bytes` / `wireguard_disk_total_bytes`, or a used-percent gauge) — reuse the source already computed for the high-disk alert. Do not build a second disk reader.
- Metrics must reflect current state at scrape time (pull model): implement a collector (or update gauges on the existing poller tick) so scraped values match what the UI shows — avoid a second, independently-drifting bookkeeping path.
- No authentication added — `/metrics` inherits the dashboard's VPN-only access model (bound to the tunnel, reachable only by tunnel members), consistent with the rest of the dashboard. Document that scraping must originate from within the tunnel.
- Tests: add a unit/HTTP test asserting `/metrics` returns 200 with the Prometheus content type and contains the expected metric families (at least one `wireguard_*` family plus the disk metric). Preserve the offline / zero-external-asset constraint — `client_golang` is a Go module dependency only, no external/runtime assets.
- Reconcile the docs: the README feature line already claims this endpoint — make it accurate (endpoint path, what it exposes, tunnel-only scraping model). Run `update_project_context` for the dashboard/server route afterward.
important_constraints:
- Dashboard-only change (Go app under `dashboard/`). Do NOT touch `terraform/`, `mcp/`, or the MCP tool surface.
- Pin `prometheus/client_golang` to an EXACT version in `dashboard/go.mod`; confirm the current version via MCP (`context7`/module releases) before pinning — do not guess from training data.
- Keep the binary a single static CGO-off build; no new external or runtime assets (holds the dashboard's offline constraint).
- Read the dashboard's `server` (HTTP handler registration) and `poller`/`serverinfo` packages before adding code; follow their existing patterns rather than introducing new ones.
- Deployment note (informational, NOT code for this task): shipping to the live host requires cutting a new dashboard release tag and bumping `dashboard_release_tag` in `terraform/dev/locals.tf` (the instance pulls the pinned tag at boot). The owner does that separately — do not run terraform.
- Open decisions to confirm with the owner if they affect scope: exact metric name set/labels, and whether alert state is one gauge-per-alert or a single labeled family.
